name: toc-container
role: Outer container for the table of contents. Sets the position variant (sticky-side / inline-top / floating-overlay), the depth (h1-h2 / h1-h3 / h1-h6), and the active-section detection root.

semanticStructure: <nav data-pattern='toc-container' data-position='sticky-side' data-depth='h1-h3' aria-label='Table of contents'>

positionContracts.sticky-side: Sidebar that sticks alongside the prose; position: sticky; top: stickyTopOffset; max-height: calc(100vh - 2 * stickyTopOffset); overflow-y: auto. Typical width 14–18rem in a two-column page layout.
positionContracts.inline-top: Inline block above the prose; collapsible by default. Used when there is no sidebar real estate (mobile / narrow content).
positionContracts.floating-overlay: Fixed-position overlay anchored to a viewport corner. Toggle via collapse-toggle. Useful for docs-site templates where the TOC is supplementary.

meta.intersectionObserverContract: Inline script (or progressive enhancement layer) attaches an IntersectionObserver to the heading elements within the prose. When a heading crosses the rootMargin threshold, the corresponding toc-item gets data-current='true' AND aria-current='location'. Default rootMargin: '0px 0px -70% 0px' (heading considered active when entering the top 30% of viewport).

ariaPatterns.navLandmark: Use a single <nav aria-label='Table of contents'> per page. Multiple TOCs require disambiguating labels (e.g., 'Documentation contents', 'Page contents').
ariaPatterns.ariaCurrentLocation: When a section is active, set aria-current='location' on its link (NOT aria-current='page' — that's reserved for the page-level navigation indicator).

meta.halt: If the consumer needs scroll-spy semantics beyond IntersectionObserver (e.g., explicit scroll progress percentage), that's a separate primitive deferred from v1.0.

name: toc-list
role: The actual ordered list of TOC entries. Composed from prim-sequence's ordered-list variant — this pack does NOT inline its own ordered-list rendering.

meta.primSequenceComposition: Always render with data-pattern='prim-sequence' data-variant='ordered-list' (or 'unordered-list' if the consumer prefers no numbering). The variant axis lives in prim-sequence; this pack delegates list semantics entirely.
meta.nestingForDepth: For data-depth='h1-h3' or deeper, nest <ol> elements inside <li data-pattern='toc-item'> to preserve heading hierarchy. The nested list inherits prim-sequence's nested-list rendering.

name: toc-item
role: One TOC entry. Wraps an anchor link to a heading element by id. Sets data-current='true' when its target heading is the active section.

ariaPatterns.ariaCurrent: When data-current='true', the inner <a> gets aria-current='location'. Set/unset together — the data attribute drives the visual; aria attribute drives AT.
ariaPatterns.indentForLevel: Visual indent comes from the parent <ol> nesting, NOT from a separate padding-per-level. Keep the inset cue purely structural.

name: active-indicator
role: Visual cue showing which section is currently in view. Rendered as a border-inline-start on the active toc-item (and optionally a fill colour change). Has no markup of its own; styling is applied to toc-item via the data-current attribute selector.

meta.implementationNote: active-indicator is NOT a separate DOM element — it's a styling concern applied to toc-item[data-current='true']. The 'style' attribute on the indicator is set at the toc-container level (e.g., data-indicator-style='border') and selectors target [data-current='true'] within that container.

name: toc-scroll-tracker
role: Conditional slot for visualizing scroll progress through the article. Only present when the operator opts in. Hidden from AT — purely decorative reinforcement of the IntersectionObserver-driven active section.

meta.ariaHidden: Always aria-hidden='true'. The IntersectionObserver-driven aria-current on toc-item provides the semantic active-section signal; this is purely decorative.
meta.implementationNote: Progress can be computed from the active-section index / total-sections, or from window.scrollY / document.body.scrollHeight. Either approach works; the first ties more tightly to the IO observer.

name: toc-collapse-toggle
role: Conditional slot for collapsing the TOC. Only present when toc-container has data-collapsible='true'. Toggles aria-expanded on itself; CSS hides/shows the list based on aria-expanded.

ariaPatterns.ariaExpanded: Boolean string ('true' / 'false'). Reflects the current state of the controlled list.
ariaPatterns.ariaControls: References the id of the toc-list element.
